angle: What umbrella insurance actually covers in 2026
hook: Your $300K liability cap won't survive one bad lawsuit.
caption_body:
Your $300K liability cap won't survive one bad lawsuit.

Umbrella policies stack on top of your auto and home liability and kick in once those run out. $1M of coverage typically costs $150–$300/year — cheaper than most streaming bundles.

Three things people get wrong:
1. They think their auto policy is enough.
2. They confuse 'underlying limits' with deductibles.
3. They skip it because they 'don't have assets' — future wages are an asset.

This is general info, not advice. Talk to a licensed agent before buying.

Save this if you're comparing quotes this month.
